Great plays from NBA Finals Game 1s since 2000"},{"url":"https://www.legit.ng/sports/1711170-they-moved-schedule-avoid-world-cup-everything-2026-nba-finals/","title":"They moved the schedule to avoid the World Cup — everything to know about the 2026 NBA Finals - Legit.ng","content":"All about the 2026 NBA Finals schedule
On 20 January 2026, the NBA made a rare change to the 2026 Finals schedule.
For the 2025–26 NBA season, the Finals are uniquely scheduled to take place from 3 June 2026 to 19 June 2026 (if a Game 7 is necessary).
The 2026 NBA Finals will air exclusively on ABC in the United States, marking the 24th consecutive year the network has served as the sole broadcaster of the championship series.
The best-of-seven championship series, broadcast exclusively on ABC, will tip off on 3 June, with a potential Game 7 scheduled for Friday, June 19."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_NBA_Finals","title":"2026 NBA Finals - Wikipedia","content":"The series was a rematch of the 1999 NBA Finals, which the Spurs won in five games, and of the 2025 NBA Cup championship game, which the Knicks won 124–113.
As the two-seed in the Western Conference playoffs, they defeated the Portland Trail Blazers in five games in the first round and the Minnesota Timberwolves in the Western Conference semifinals in six games.
The league rearranged the series so that it avoided conflicting with the 2026 FIFA World Cup night game between the United States and Paraguay on June 12.
The Knicks became the third team in NBA Finals history to win the first two games on the road, after the 1993 Chicago Bulls and the 1995 Houston Rockets.
They also had the highest average margin of win at plus-14.9, surpassing the 1970-71 Milwaukee Bucks (14.5).
The 14-year drought between championships was the longest in the city's history since their drought from 1962 to 1969.
Jalen Brunson became the eighth player to win the NBA Finals MVP after winning an NCAA Division I men's basketball tournament and fourth of those to have also won state high school championship, joining Kareem Abdul-Jabbar, Bill Walton, and Magic Johnson.
"NBA adjusts Finals schedule; no Sunday games for first time since 1970"."},{"url":"https://payperhead.com/nba/nba-finals/","title":"NBA Finals 2026: Schedule, Scores, and Championship History","content":"The 2026 NBA Finals schedule will begin on Wednesday, June 3, 2026, and could extend to Friday, June 19 if the series goes to seven games.
One major change this year is that the NBA avoided scheduling Sunday games during the Finals because of the 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule.
Fans looking for where to watch the NBA Finals 2026 will be able to follow the championship series through several major broadcasters and streaming platforms.
Fans constantly search for the latest NBA Finals scores to follow every possession, momentum shift, and game-changing performance during the championship series.
Historic Finals performances featured dominant scoring, triple-doubles, defensive excellence, and unforgettable closeout games that defined entire careers."}]}

The schedule was released on Tuesday and is as follows:. Game 2: Friday, June 5. Game 5: Saturday, June 13 (if needed). Game 6: Tuesday, June 16 (if needed). Game 7: Friday, June 19 (if needed). The major change is that none of the NBA Finals games have a Sunday worked anywhere in there. That's the first time since 1970 that there won't be any Finals games on a Sunday. One item of note is that there will only be one day of travel between Games 1 and 2, which goes against the last 10 years of NBA Finals. The Oklahoma City Thunder won the 2025 NBA Finals in seven games over the Indiana Pacers.Justin Ford / Getty Images. The NBA announced the schedule for the 2026 NBA Finals on Tuesday, and the series will follow a slightly different pattern from the way it had been scheduled in recent years. The finals will start on a Wednesday (June 3) as opposed to 2025, when the series between the Oklahoma City Thunder and Indiana Pacers began on a Thursday. According to Sports Media Watch, 2026 will be the first year since 1970 that no NBA Finals game will be scheduled on a Sunday. According to Sports Media Watch, the NBA would typically play Game 4 of the finals on Friday, June 12, but the league instead scheduled the game for Wednesday, June 10. Game 2 will be played on Friday, June 5.","score":0.86441374,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.9,"request_id":"e0acf176-4744-4eeb-b9d8-30d83612f92a"}
